sentence_reviews.text: A learner maps inputs to labeled targets using an explicit error signal.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', including 19.1 'Forms of Learning', 19.2 'Supervised Learning', and 19.3 'Learning Decision Trees' (lines 488-508).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA table of contents directly identifies supervised learning as learning from examples; the sentence is a concise definition of that setup.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Supervised learning is to connect Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences to downstream capabilities including Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Preference learning, Active learning & curriculum learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523) and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports supervised learning, examples, reinforcement learning, and active learning topics, but the exact graph-bridge claim and listed downstream set are atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', especially 19.2 'Supervised Learning' and 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 488-516).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports supervised learning and less-data learning as related topics; 'substrate for' is editorial graph wording.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523) and Chapter 17 'Making Complex Decisions' (lines 418-439).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports learning and decision/utility topics, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish preference learning as downstream of supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Active learning & curriculum learning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6.5 'Active learning' (line 516) and Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523).
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning is directly named under learning from examples; curriculum learning is not directly named in the inspected source.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' and 19.7 'Nonparametric Models' (lines 488-523), plus Chapter 27 'Philosophy, Ethics, and Safety of AI' (lines 731-763).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports learning from data/examples and broader AI limitations, but not the deployment-objective mismatch wording or dependency list exactly.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised learning uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' and 19.7 'Nonparametric Models' (lines 488-523).
sentence_reviews.notes: Learning from examples implies data use; the split/sampling detail is an atlas-level elaboration.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised learning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.2 'Supervised Learning' (line 493) and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: Labels/targets are supported for supervised learning, but rewards and preferences broaden the statement beyond the supervised-learning evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Supervised learning uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 4.1 'Local Search and Optimization Problems' (lines 103-109), Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523), and Chapter 21 'Deep Learning' (lines 538-552).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports optimization and supervised/deep learning as related AI topics, but does not state this dependency verbatim.

edge_reviews.notes: Supervised learning from examples depends on data examples; sampling/splits are reasonable but not directly named in the inspected evidence.
The supervised-learning setup requires target/label information, though the dependency label also includes broader reward/preference signals.
Optimization is commonly used in learning algorithms; AIMA supports both areas but not the exact edge statement.
